example: She is an ambitious young entrepreneur who wants to change the world.
They had to negotiate the terms of the contract for several weeks.
The company switched to sustainable packaging to reduce waste.
According to the forecast, it will rain all weekend.
I am so grateful for all the help you gave me.
He dedicated his life to helping others in need.
hint: Always with 'to': 'dedicate time to', 'dedicate yourself to'.

example: Social media has a huge influence on modern culture.
After the long flight, she was completely exhausted.
She accomplished all her goals by the end of the year.
He felt anxious before his job interview.
hint: 'Anxious about' = worried. Or eager: 'anxious to help'.

example: We need to stick to our budget this month.
Try to look at the problem from a different perspective.
hint: 'From my perspective' — polite way to share your view.

example: Teachers should encourage students to ask questions.
Many people now work in remote positions from home.
It is wise to invest your savings rather than keep them in cash.
hint: Always 'invest in': 'invest in stocks', 'invest in yourself'.

example: You need a reliable car if you drive long distances every day.
hint: For people, machines and data: 'a reliable source'.

example: The outcome of the meeting was better than expected.
hint: More formal than 'result' — the final effect of a process.

example: The old bridge collapsed after years of neglect.
hint: Literal (buildings) or figurative: a deal, economy can collapse.

example: After six months she was offered a permanent position.
There is a serious shortage of affordable housing in the city.
hint: 'A shortage of' — supply less than demand.

example: The demand for electric cars is growing every year.
Don't hesitate to call me if you need any help.
hint: 'Don't hesitate to...' = 'feel free to...' — common in emails.

example: She made a conscious decision to eat more healthily.
hint: Don't confuse with 'conscience' — your moral sense.

example: The company plans to launch a new product next spring.
hint: For products, campaigns, websites — starting something publicly.

example: Her smile was genuine, and everyone could feel her warmth.
hint: Opposite of fake. Also: 'genuine leather', 'genuine signature'.

example: Every obstacle is an opportunity to learn something new.
